title: brunellesiales
intro: The Brunellesiales is an order of flowering plants, ranking below the subclass and above the family in the taxonomic hierarchy. It is a division of flowering plants characterized by shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The Brunellesiales order includes various plant families, such as the Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, the Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and poinsettia, and the Cyperaceae, which comprises sedge and rush plants. These families share similar morphological and genetic traits that distinguish them from other plant orders.

families.family_desc: Brunelliaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the paperbark trees, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
